claim: Haiku 4.5 is a third of the price of Sonnet 4.5, allowing you to run three Haiku prompts for the cost of one Sonnet prompt
actionable: For cost-sensitive agentic tasks, systematically try Haiku 4.5 first before defaulting to Sonnet to maximize budget efficiency.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 runs approximately a second faster than Sonnet 4.5 with 10% more tool calls in the same time period
actionable: Use Haiku 4.5 for latency-sensitive agentic coding tasks where throughput of tool calls per unit time matters more than individual result quality.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 reports 100+ to 200+ tokens per second throughput once it gets going, compared to slower models
actionable: Choose Haiku for scenarios where response speed and throughput are critical, such as real-time agent interactions or iterative front-end coding.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 missed 1 out of 32 files (3% failure rate) in a file summarization task while Sonnet found all files correctly
actionable: Use Sonnet for mission-critical tasks requiring 100% accuracy; reserve Haiku for scenarios where a 3% error rate is acceptable trade-off for speed and cost.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 loses detail and misses instructions compared to Sonnet, particularly on complex multi-step prompts requiring deep contextual understanding
actionable: When using Haiku for complex tasks, simplify and break down multi-step instructions into separate, focused prompts to compensate for detail loss.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 violated explicit instructions (not to use sub-agents) while Sonnet followed the instruction correctly
actionable: When using Haiku for constrained tasks with explicit restrictions, add redundant checks or validation steps since Haiku may not reliably follow negative instructions.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 is not a good planner; it works fast but produces shorter, less detailed plans compared to Sonnet
actionable: Use Sonnet for planning phases of multi-step projects, then use Haiku for execution and implementation of those plans.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 successfully completed a feature implementation task (regex search, timer, UI themes) in about 3 minutes using 25 tool calls
actionable: For straightforward feature implementation work, rely on Haiku's speed to rapidly prototype and build features without full Sonnet capabilities.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: Haiku 4.5 performed documentation search and summarization tasks comparably to Sonnet, suggesting it excels at simpler work
actionable: Deploy Haiku for document summarization, code search, and simple retrieval tasks; save Sonnet for complex reasoning and design work.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: The right way to use coding agents is to iterate quickly with short prompts, kill agents if they fail, and avoid complex multi-stage planning
actionable: When using Haiku or any model for agentic coding, favor rapid iteration with simple prompts over elaborate upfront planning.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding

claim: A model stack (weak, base, strong like Haiku, Sonnet, Opus) is critical for scaling agents and trading off price, performance, and speed
actionable: Build your agent infrastructure to support multiple model tiers, routing simple tasks to Haiku and complex ones to Sonnet or Opus.
source_title: Claude HAIKU 4.5 is LIGHT SPEED Agentic Coding
